pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 17 and export_result.sh line 12
Task description explicitly states '0.50 micrometers (µm) per pixel'

Analysis of Blobs (25K) image with 0.5 µm/px calibration shows calibrated areas range from ~2 µm² (noise particles) to ~245 µm² at optimal threshold. Value of 20 filters out noise particles; verified by simulation and verifier logic at line 70

Verifier.py comment (lines 9-10) states 'Calibrated (0.5µm/px) Area ~ 40-600 µm²'. Simulation of Blobs (25K) image shows max calibrated area ~245 µm² at optimal threshold (100), well below 600. The metadata value of 700 contradicts the verifier's documented expected range of 40-600 µm².
Verifier.py line 34 uses this to distinguish between calibrated measurements (~40-600 µm²) and uncalibrated measurements (~160-2400 px²). Threshold of 1000 is valid cutoff between these ranges
Simulation of Blobs (25K) particle analysis with 0.5 µm/px calibration yields 65 particles after noise filtering, well above the minimum threshold of 20. Verifier.py line 62-66 implements this check

privileged_info_summary: The task requires spatial calibration of the Blobs (25K) sample image (a 256x254 pixel synthetic test image built into Fiji) to 0.5 micrometers per pixel, then measurement of segmented particles. With proper calibration and analysis at a reasonable threshold (e.g., intensity > 100), calibrated blob areas range from approximately 20-245 µm² with median ~98 µm², and 65+ particles are detected. The expected output file is /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/calibrated_blob_measurements.csv. The Blobs (25K) sample is built into Fiji and requires no external data download—it is a synthetic image available via File > Open Samples.
